skill.name: meeting-notes
skill.description: Convert meeting discussions into clear, actionable notes with tasks, decisions, owners, due dates, follow-ups, and reusable meeting formats.
skill.summary: Create structured meeting notes with decisions, action items, follow-ups, and templates.
skill.author: CuriousLearner

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged command execution, hardcoded URLs, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance-like terms in SKILL.md. Manual review found these are Markdown templates, example resource links, and meeting-note content, with no executable code, network calls, credential handling, or prompt injection evidence.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Command Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The detected backtick patterns are Markdown code fences around note templates, metrics, and usage examples. They are not Ruby or shell execution and do not run commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are Markdown fence delimiters and template examples. No shell interpreter, Ruby execution context, command arguments, or user-controlled execution path exists.

low_findings.title: Example Links Flagged as Network Activity
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The hardcoded URLs are illustrative links in meeting-note resource sections. The skill does not fetch, post, or transmit data to these addresses.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only inside example note output. There is no network API, command, or instruction that would access them automatically.

low_findings.title: Meeting Template Terms Flagged as Blocker Patterns
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The weak-cryptography and reconnaissance alerts match ordinary meeting-note content, such as action owners, API documentation, deployment planning, and team templates. No evidence found of cryptographic implementation, host discovery, port scanning, or data exfiltration intent.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed context is meeting documentation and project planning text. The file contains no runnable scanner, cryptographic code, network probing command, or suspicious authority claim.

content.value_statement: Meeting discussions often lose decisions, owners, and deadlines after the call ends. This skill turns raw discussion notes into structured summaries, action items, decision logs, and follow-up plans.
actual_capabilities: Formats meeting notes with attendees, agenda, discussion summaries, decisions, and follow-up sections.
Extracts action items with owners, due dates, dependencies, priorities, and acceptance criteria.
Creates templates for standard meetings, sprint planning, standups, and retrospectives.
Highlights decisions with rationale, impact, owner, timeline, and next steps.
Organizes questions, parking lot topics, related resources, and notes for the next meeting.
limitations: Requires meeting text, transcript excerpts, or user-provided context to produce accurate notes.
Cannot verify attendee names, dates, decisions, or commitments without reliable source material.
Does not automatically update external tools such as Jira, Slack, Google Docs, or Confluence.
May need review before sharing notes that contain sensitive business or personal information.

use_cases.title: Document Team Meetings
use_cases.description: Turn discussion notes into a clear record with decisions, action items, and follow-up topics.

use_cases.title: Run Agile Ceremonies
use_cases.description: Create sprint planning, standup, review, and retrospective notes with owners and priorities.

use_cases.title: Share Executive Updates
use_cases.description: Summarize stakeholder meetings into concise outcomes, risks, decisions, and next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Summarize a Meeting
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the meeting-notes skill to summarize these rough notes. Include attendees, agenda, discussion summary, decisions, action items, and follow-ups.

prompt_templates.title: Extract Action Items
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused request for owners and deadlines.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the meeting-notes skill to extract action items from this transcript. Include owner, due date, priority, dependencies, and acceptance criteria for each item.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Decision Log
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the meeting-notes skill to create a decision log from these notes. Capture each decision, rationale, impact, decision owner, and required next step.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Follow-Up Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the meeting-notes skill to convert this meeting transcript into an execution plan. Separate immediate tasks, blocked items, open questions, risks, and review checkpoints.

output_examples.input: A product meeting discussed launch readiness, unresolved QA issues, marketing copy, and a support training deadline.
output_examples.output: The result is a structured meeting record with a launch decision, QA blockers, named owners for copy and training, due dates, and follow-up review items.

output_examples.input: A sprint retrospective covered missed scope, one production bug, improved code reviews, and a need for better API documentation.
output_examples.output: The result is a retrospective summary with what went well, what needs improvement, experiments for the next sprint, and prioritized action items.

output_examples.input: A weekly standup included completed work, current priorities, blockers, and requests for help from several team members.
output_examples.output: The result is a concise standup note that groups progress, current work, blockers, support requests, and follow-up assignments by owner.

best_practices: Provide raw notes or transcript excerpts with speaker names when possible.
Review action items with attendees before sending notes to a wider audience.
Ask for separate sections for decisions, risks, open questions, and follow-ups.
anti_patterns: Do not use it to invent decisions, owners, or deadlines that were not discussed.
Do not share sensitive meeting content before reviewing the generated notes.
Do not treat the output as a source of truth without confirming unclear points.

faq.question: Can this skill summarize raw transcripts?
faq.answer: Yes. It can turn transcript text into structured meeting notes, but speaker names and timestamps improve accuracy.

faq.question: Can it create action items?
faq.answer: Yes. It can extract tasks, owners, due dates, dependencies, priorities, and acceptance criteria from provided meeting content.

faq.question: Does it update Jira, Slack, or Google Docs?
faq.answer: No. It produces text that users can copy into external tools, but it does not update those tools automatically.

faq.question: What meeting types does it support?
faq.answer: It supports general meetings, sprint planning, standups, retrospectives, decision logs, and follow-up planning.

faq.question: Can it handle confidential meetings?
faq.answer: It can structure confidential notes, but users should review and redact sensitive details before sharing.

faq.question: Does it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
